questions.question: Which type of mirror is most suitable for focusing sunlight to produce heat?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors converge light rays to a focal point, concentrating heat.

questions.question: Which mirror always produces a virtual, upright, and smaller image?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always form virtual, upright, and diminished images.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles?

options.B: They provide a wider field of view

questions.explanation: Convex mirrors diverge light rays and allow drivers to see a larger area.

questions.question: Which lens converges parallel light rays to a single focal point?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses bring parallel light rays together at the focus.

questions.question: Which lens always forms a virtual and smaller image of an object?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses diverge light rays and always form virtual, smaller images.

questions.question: What happens to light when it passes through a convex lens from a distant object?

options.B: It converges at the focal point

questions.explanation: Convex lenses converge parallel rays at the focus.

questions.question: Which mirror can produce both real and virtual images depending on the object's position?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors can produce real or virtual images depending on object distance.

questions.question: Which optical instrument uses mirrors to observe objects from behind obstacles?
questions.explanation: Periscopes use mirrors to reflect light and allow viewing from hidden positions.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror produce a magnified image when the object is placed close to it?

options.C: Because reflected rays diverge from a virtual point

questions.explanation: When the object is inside the focal length, the reflected rays diverge and appear to come from a larger virtual image.

questions.question: Which phenomenon explains why objects appear bent in water?
questions.explanation: Light bends when it passes between different media, causing refraction.

questions.question: Which lens is used in cameras to focus light onto film or sensors?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses focus light to form clear images in cameras.

questions.question: Which property of light explains the formation of images in mirrors?
questions.explanation: Images form in mirrors due to reflection of light.

questions.question: Why do security mirrors in shops use convex mirrors?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors provide a wide field of view.

questions.question: Which type of lens is used in magnifying glasses?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses enlarge objects by converging light rays.

questions.question: Which mirror produces images that appear behind the mirror surface?
questions.explanation: Both plane and convex mirrors form virtual images behind the mirror.

questions.question: Which optical instrument uses lenses to observe very distant stars?
questions.explanation: Telescopes use lenses or mirrors to observe distant celestial objects.

questions.question: Why does a concave lens always produce a smaller image?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses spread light rays, forming smaller virtual images.

questions.question: Which property of light allows mirrors to form images?
questions.explanation: Light reflects off mirror surfaces to form images.

questions.question: Which mirror can concentrate sunlight at a single point?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors converge parallel rays at the focal point.

questions.question: What type of image does a convex mirror form?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors form virtual and upright images.

questions.question: Which lens is used to correct short-sightedness?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses diverge light rays to correct short-sightedness.

questions.question: Why does a plane mirror produce a virtual image?

options.A: Light rays actually meet behind the mirror
options.B: Reflected rays appear to come from behind the mirror

questions.explanation: Reflected rays appear to originate behind the mirror, forming a virtual image.

questions.question: Which law states that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection?
questions.explanation: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection.

questions.question: Which device uses lenses to view extremely small objects?
questions.explanation: Microscopes use lenses to magnify very small objects.

questions.question: Which factor determines whether a concave mirror forms a real or virtual image?
questions.explanation: Image type depends on the object's position relative to the focal point.
